name: Mirador de los Balcones de Madrid – circular desde Parada de Sil – Ribeira Sacra
region_intro: Jogging around Parada De Sil offers a variety of routes through a landscape characterized by river canyons, dense forests, and elevated viewpoints. The region's terrain includes significant elevation changes, providing challenging running experiences. Many routes follow established paths, offering scenic viewpoints Parada De Sil jogging opportunities over the Sil River. The area is suitable for runners seeking varied distances and elevations. Best jogging routes around Parada De Sil The most popular jogging route is Monasterio de San Pedro de Rocas – circular by the Camino Real, a 4.8 miles (7.7 km) trail that takes 1 hour 1 minute to complete. This moderate route features a mix of forest paths and historical sites. Another top favourite among local runners is Mirador de los Balcones de Madrid – circular desde Parada de Sil – Ribeira Sacra, a difficult 11.1 miles (17.9 km) path. This route offers expansive views from the Balcones de Madrid viewpoint. Local runners also love the Ribeira Sacra Natural Trail, an 11.2 miles (18.0 km) trail leading through the natural landscapes of Ribeira Sacra, often completed in about 2 hours 20 minutes. Jogging around Parada De Sil is defined by river valleys, forested slopes, and elevated viewpoints. The network offers options for different ability levels, with a focus on moderate to difficult running routes. The routes in Parada De Sil are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from 1 review. More than 160 runners have used komoot to explore Parada De Sil's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes can I find in Parada De Sil?
A: There are over 20 dedicated running routes around Parada De Sil, offering a variety of experiences through the region's unique landscape. Most of these routes are considered moderate to difficult due to the significant elevation changes.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly jogging paths in Parada De Sil?
A: While many routes in Parada De Sil feature challenging elevation changes, there are some moderate options suitable for those looking for a less strenuous run. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990556161/monasterio-de-san-pedro-de-rocas-circular-by-the-camino-real" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monasterio de San Pedro de Rocas – circular by the Camino Real&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 4.8-mile trail that offers a mix of forest paths and historical sites.

Q: What kind of distances and elevations can I expect on the running routes?
A: Running routes in Parada De Sil vary significantly in distance and elevation. You can find trails ranging from around 4.8 miles (7.7 km) with moderate climbs, to longer, more challenging routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990597251/ribeira-sacra-natural-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ribeira Sacra Natural Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which is 11.2 miles (18.0 km) long with over 600 meters of elevation gain. The terrain is characterized by river canyons and forested slopes, ensuring varied and often demanding runs.

Q: Are there any circular jogging routes in the area?
A: Yes, many of the running routes in Parada De Sil are circular, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990549822/mirador-de-los-balcones-de-madrid-circular-desde-parada-de-sil-ribeira-sacra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de los Balcones de Madrid – circular desde Parada de Sil – Ribeira Sacra&lt;/a&gt;, a challenging 11.1-mile loop offering expansive views.

Q: What scenic viewpoints can I discover while running in Parada De Sil?
A: Parada De Sil is renowned for its stunning viewpoints over the Sil River canyons. Along your runs, you can encounter breathtaking spots such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4576799" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Balcones de Madrid Viewpoint (Os Torgás Viewpoint)&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3726236" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cividade Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4213863" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pedra Longa Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, offering panoramic vistas of the Ribeira Sacra landscape.

Q: Are there any historical or natural landmarks to see along the running trails?
A: Absolutely. The region is rich in history and natural beauty. You can run past the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3136768" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monastery of San Pedro de Rocas&lt;/a&gt;, explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3852882" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mao River Boardwalk&lt;/a&gt;, or discover the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4624267" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Vítor de Barxacova Chapel and Necropolis&lt;/a&gt;. Many trails also lead through ancient chestnut groves, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6125209" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chestnut Trail in Parada de Sil&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Parada De Sil?
A: The running routes in Parada De Sil are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.0 stars. Runners frequently praise the stunning natural scenery, the challenging yet rewarding terrain, and the opportunity to explore both river valleys and elevated viewpoints.

Q: Is Parada De Sil suitable for family-friendly running adventures?
A: While many routes feature significant elevation, some shorter, moderate trails can be enjoyed by active families. It's advisable to check the specific route details for distance and elevation to ensure it matches your family's fitness level. The area's natural beauty offers a great outdoor experience for all.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the jogging trails in Parada De Sil?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on trails in natural areas around Parada De Sil, especially if kept on a leash. However, it's always best to check local signage or specific route information for any restrictions, particularly in protected areas or near historical sites, to ensure a pleasant experience for everyone.

Q: Where can I find parking for the running routes in Parada De Sil?
A: Parking is typically available near the starting points of many popular trails and in the village of Parada De Sil itself. Specific parking information can often be found in the detailed descriptions of individual routes on komoot, or by checking local maps for designated parking areas.

Q: What is the best time of year for running in Parada De Sil?
A: The spring and autumn months generally offer the most pleasant conditions for running in Parada De Sil, with milder temperatures and vibrant natural colors. Summer can be warm, especially in the canyons, while winter may bring cooler temperatures and occasional rain, though many routes remain accessible.

Q: Are there any cafes or places to refuel near the jogging routes?
A: Parada De Sil is a small village, and you'll find a few local establishments where you can refuel before or after your run. It's a good idea to carry water and snacks, especially on longer routes, as amenities might be sparse directly along the trails in this rural region.
tips:
The Torgás recreational area is in an area where a bush called heather abounds, it is also close to leafy groves of century-old chestnut trees.
Mountainous hiking area, with viewpoints that offer panoramic views of a river valley.

Miradoiro dos Penediños is a beautiful viewing point located in the Ribeira Sacra, a region known for its stunning landscapes and terraced vineyards along the Miño and Sil rivers in Galicia. From this viewing point, you can enjoy spectacular panoramic views of the Miño River canyons and the lush forests surrounding the area.
The viewing point is an ideal place for nature and photography lovers, as it offers a unique perspective of the natural beauty of the Ribeira Sacra. In addition, it is a popular stopping point for visitors touring the region's viewing point route.
